intro: Parosela elata, also known as the Brazilian star cluster, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Parosela elata include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
